principles.title: Reuse before Buy before Build
principles.description: Consider reusing existing solutions before purchasing new ones and only consider building new solutions as a last resort.
principles.rationale:
- Improves cost efficiency, reduces complexity and time to market
- Packaged solutions are well tested and based on market standards
- Highly customized solutions lead to high maintenance costs
principles.implications:
- Always look at existing solutions within the company first to solve a new business need
- Architectural components should be centralized and designed for reuse in order to minimize the cost of purchasing/developing new ones
- If opting for a packaged solution, limit customization to less than 30% of the package's overall functionality

principles.title: Prefer fully managed Services
principles.description: Prioritize the adoption of fully managed services over self-managed infrastructure solutions (SaaS before PaaS, before IaaS).
principles.rationale:
- Reduces operational burden as managed services offer built-in maintenance, support and scalability
- Accelerates time-to-market and fosters innovation by freeing up internal resources
principles.implications:
- Always scout the market for fully managed services (SaaS) to solve a business need
- Opt for PaaS when SaaS solutions are unavailable in the market or deemed unsuitable due to compliance considerations
- Utilize IaaS sparingly, reserving it as a fallback option, while ensuring a strategy is in place for ultimately transitioning towards PaaS or SaaS

principles.title: Business value-driven Architecture
principles.description: All architecture decisions should be driven by business drivers and goals.
principles.rationale:
- Drives business value
- Avoid the cost of implementing technology for technology's sake
- All IT investments should be aligned with business strategy and anchored to clear business objectives
- Clarifies responsibilities and ownership
principles.implications:
- Seek to understand business drivers and goals
- Link all architecture decisions to business objectives
- Always have a business case, business requirements and clear business value in mind

principles.title: One capability, One solution
principles.description: Implement one solution per business capability.
principles.rationale:
- Ensures cost efficiency and optimal use of resources by avoiding functional redundancy
- Simplifies IT portfolio and minimizes maintenance/support efforts (less applications to manage)
principles.implications:
- Always look at existing solutions within the company when addressing a new requirement
- Ensure that existing solutions cannot be utilized before implementing a new solution

principles.title: Exit Strategy
principles.description: Have an exit strategy in place when selecting solutions.
principles.rationale:
- Avoid vendor/solution lock-in
- Acknowledges the dynamic nature of business strategies and tech landscapes where solutions may need to be replaced or retired over time
- Mitigates risks associated with vendor discontinuations, EOL scenarios, pricing or changes in business/technical needs
principles.implications:
- Develop data migration strategies and contingency plans to accommodate for exit scenarios
- Regularly review and update exit strategies to align with evolving business needs and technology landscapes

principles.title: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)
principles.description: Consider all direct and indirect costs associated with acquiring, implementing, operating and maintaining technology solutions throughout their lifecycle.
principles.rationale:
- Ensures that overall solution costs have been considered for value for money
- The initial purchase cost of a technology solution represents only a fraction of its total cost over its entire lifecycle
- TCO analysis provides a more comprehensive assessment of the financial implications and long-term value of IT-related investments
principles.implications:
- Analyze and document thoroughly all financial implications when selecting solutions
- Consider alternative solutions or deployment models based on TCO

principles.title: Seamless Integration
principles.description: Prioritize solutions that offer seamless integration capabilities out of the box, utilizing open protocols and standards.
principles.rationale:
- Facilitates interoperability with existing solutions
- Minimizes the need for custom development and reduces integration complexity
- Enables (OOTB) efficient data exchange across diverse technology landscapes
principles.implications:
- Assess integration capabilities considering support for open standards such as HTTP, REST etc.
- Leverage pre-built connectors, adapters and integration patterns for commonly used solutions

principles.title: Technical Debt
principles.description: Be mindful of the impact that accumulated technical debt has and prioritize solutions with limited technical debt impact.
principles.rationale:
- Promotes architecture standardization and consistency
- Reduces architectural complexity and maintainability efforts
- Supports long-term cost savings and agility by reducing the need for refactoring and rework
principles.implications:
- Be mindful of what constitutes technical debt
- Thoroughly evaluate solutions for potential technical debt
- Track and monitor technical debt over time

principles.title: Legal & Regulatory Compliance
principles.description: All solutions must adhere to applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
principles.rationale:
- Ensures compliance with relevant laws, standards and industry regulations
- Mitigates legal risks and protects sensitive data
- Supports integrity, accountability and responsible business practices
principles.implications:
- Engage legal and compliance experts
- Thoroughly evaluate solutions against relevant legal and regulatory frameworks

principles.title: Security by Design
principles.description: All solutions must adhere to the enterprise's security and cybersecurity standards.
principles.rationale:
- Mitigates security/cybersecurity risks and safeguards the organization's assets
- Security measures implemented during design are more effective and cost-efficient than retroactive security fixes applied after deployment
principles.implications:
- Embed security requirements into solution design from the outset rather than bolting them on later
- Engage security experts during architecture and solution design reviews
- Continuously assess solutions against the enterprise's security and cybersecurity standards throughout their lifecycle
